context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=29; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user consistently leaves cold fruit meals unheated. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals center on moderate reheating. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE the observed personal sandwich meal used the hottest reheating level. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE both left- and right-social fruit meals were served without reheating. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed left-social savory and sandwich meals used moderate reheating. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized savory meal such as pizza and broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE the earlier front-social savory meal used moderate reheating. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal with dip such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing THEN 1 min BECAUSE the newer front-social savory meal used lighter reheating than the user's other savory front-social meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit-and-dessert meal with chocolate sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user warmed that sweet meal rather than treating it like cold fruit. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE repeated sandwich meals in that context used reheating. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce, or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals consistently centered on moderate reheating. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or chicken breast strips and hash brown THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals consistently favor the hottest observed reheating level. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the newly reinforced left-laptop fruit meal matched the user's broader cold-fruit pattern. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as chicken nuggets, chicken breast strips, hash brown, broccoli, or ketchup-accompanied items THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE left-laptop savory meals consistently used reheating. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-laptop observations of the same savory meal both used reheating, with the newer choice softening from 3 min to 2 min. Across observed meals, fruit is usually not microwaved, while savory, sandwich, and dessert-containing meals are commonly reheated, typically at moderate levels, with sandwiches and front-TV savory meals sometimes favoring 3 min.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in observed personal settings. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also ignored occlusion. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-social meals prioritized preserving the social partner's side. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips or fruit-with-topping meal THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-social meals prioritized preserving the social partner's side. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE both observed front-social meals prioritized preserving the front-facing social view. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals prioritized preserving the front-facing TV view. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN minimize right occlusion is the clearest tendency BECAUSE multiple right-TV meals prioritized preserving the TV view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals prioritized preserving the front-facing work view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the new left-laptop fruit meal again did not prioritize preserving the left-side work view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN usually do not consider occlusion, but context-specific variation exists BECAUSE the user selected do not consider occlusion in multiple left-laptop meals yet selected minimize left occlusion in another left-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both minimize right occlusion and do not consider occlusion selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes treats right-side work visibility as important but not uniformly across all right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, occlusion preference is direction-specific and context-specific rather than uniformly applied whenever a person or screen is present.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user previously preferred a comfortable, unhurried motion style in personal dining. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup THEN medium BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals used a somewhat faster pace. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN slow BECAUSE the personal sandwich meal returned to the more comfort-oriented pace. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium is still the best overall summary, with both slow and fast also established BECAUSE earlier social meals included slow, several social meals used medium, and the newer front-social savory meal used fast. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN medium to fast BECAUSE front-social savory meals have now used both medium and fast, showing greater willingness to speed up in that context. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN medium BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals consistently use a moderate pace. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium is the clearest tendency BECAUSE right-TV meals have repeatedly used medium alongside less frequent slow and fast selections. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN medium is favored BECAUSE the earlier front-laptop meal used slow but the newer front-laptop meal used medium. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including fruit meals without sauces or dips THEN medium is now the clearest tendency BECAUSE multiple left-laptop meals, including the new fruit meal, used medium while slow occurred less often. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with slow, medium, and fast all observed, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward efficiency in right-laptop work contexts but does not do so consistently. Across observed contexts, personal settings usually favor slower movement, while screen-based contexts and social meals show willingness to use medium speed, with occasional fast selections in efficiency-oriented or socially front-facing meals.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this is the default higher-success acquisition style and no contrary personal fruit-specific preference has been established. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals used the higher-success acquisition style. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the personal sandwich meal used the narrower bite-acquisition style. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the observed left-social sandwich meal matched the sandwich-specific narrower bite pattern. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed fruit or bite-sized savory meals other than sandwiches THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in those meals, including both front-social savory meals. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or chicken breast strips and hash brown THEN mixed evidence, with both perpendicular and parallel selected, BECAUSE one front-TV savory meal used perpendicular while a later front-TV chicken-strips meal used parallel. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit-and-dessert meal with bananas and brownies THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in that mixed sweet meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated sandwich meals in that context matched the narrower bite-acquisition preference. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meals such as chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce, or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals consistently used the higher-success acquisition style rather than the sandwich-specific parallel pattern. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the narrower bite-acquisition style in that work-context fruit meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the new left-laptop fruit meal used the narrower bite-acquisition style rather than the general fruit default. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected a narrower bite-acquisition style for that food. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both perpendicular and parallel selected, BECAUSE earlier left-laptop savory meals used perpendicular while a chicken breast strips and hash brown meal and the new fruit meal used parallel. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style for that savory work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated front-laptop fish-bite meals used the higher-success acquisition style. Across observed meals, skewering preference appears food-dependent rather than purely context-driven, with perpendicular generally favored for acquisition success but parallel used for sandwiches and some fruit or narrower-bite work-context cases.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to avoid extra confirmation overhead. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN no BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also skipped explicit confirmation. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user still chose to skip explicit bite-acquisition confirmation even in social settings, including the newer front-social savory meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition, including the new left-laptop fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reduced interaction burden and greater automation. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to interrupt the meal with extra web confirmation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers not to add this extra web confirmation step.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's selected and physically appropriate transfer mode is inside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal readiness cues. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also used multimodal readiness cues. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN LED, speech + LED, and speech are all established, with no single social cue style fully dominant BECAUSE earlier social meals used speech + LED, later left-social meals used LED alone, and the newer front-social savory meal used speech alone. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN speech is now favored BECAUSE both front-facing social salience and the newest front-social meal support speech-only cues in that context. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech is favored BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used speech-only rather than multimodal cues. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech and speech + LED are both established, with speech slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used speech-only while other right-TV meals used speech + LED. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using speech + LED and another using speech, BECAUSE the user accepts either cue style while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cues in the observed left-laptop meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in other right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED remains the overall default, while speech-only is especially acceptable in front-facing screen and front-social contexts, and LED-only is a repeated social-context option.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly preferred an active but low-effort readiness signal. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals retained voluntary low-effort control. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue is now slightly favored over open mouth BECAUSE earlier social meals used open mouth, but multiple newer social meals, including left-social and front-social meals, used autocontinue. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN autocontinue is favored BECAUSE both observed front-social savory meals used automatic initiation. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is favored BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with autocontinue slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used autocontinue while some used open mouth. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using open mouth and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward greater automation while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary low-effort signaling in all observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers highly automated initiation in that work context but also often retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and the user generally favors open mouth in personal and left-laptop work settings, with autocontinue used more often in social and front-facing screen contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and selected a low-effort voluntary readiness cue. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also used voluntary readiness signaling. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue is now slightly favored over open mouth BECAUSE earlier social meals used open mouth, but multiple newer social meals, including left-social and front-social meals, used autocontinue. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN autocontinue is favored BECAUSE both observed front-social savory meals used automatic initiation for drinking as well. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is favored BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with autocontinue slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used autocontinue while some used open mouth. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using open mouth and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward greater automation while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary readiness signaling in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers automatic progression in that work context but also sometimes retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and drinking initiation usually mirrors feeding: open mouth by default, with autocontinue used more often in social and front-facing screen contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also used automatic wiping initiation. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation, including the newer front-social savory meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition, including the new left-laptop fruit meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for mouth-wiping initiation across observed work meals. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers higher automation for wiping initiation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal completion cues. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also used multimodal completion cues. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN LED, speech + LED, and speech are all established, with no single social cue style fully dominant BECAUSE earlier social meals used speech + LED, later left-social meals used LED alone, and the newer front-social savory meal used speech alone. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN speech is now favored BECAUSE the newest front-social meal used speech-only and front-facing contexts already support speech salience. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech is favored BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used speech-only rather than multimodal cues. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech and speech + LED are both established, with speech slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used speech-only while other right-TV meals used speech + LED. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using speech + LED and another using speech, BECAUSE the user accepts either completion cue style while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only completion cues in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in other right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED remains the overall default, while speech-only is especially acceptable in front-facing screen and front-social contexts, and LED-only is a repeated social-context option.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected the reliable low-effort completion detection method for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for drinking. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN perception BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also used perception-based drinking completion. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with perception no longer clearly secondary BECAUSE earlier social meals used perception, some later social meals used autocontinue, and the newest front-social savory meal returned to perception. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and perception selected, BECAUSE the earlier front-social savory meal used autocontinue while the newer one used perception. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is favored BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with no clear winner BECAUSE some right-TV meals used autocontinue and others used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN perception BECAUSE all observed left-laptop work meals used explicit perception-based completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the observed right-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using perception and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts more automation for drinking completion while working in front of a laptop. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice overall, while autocontinue appears especially in some screen-focused and social contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for mouth wiping. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN perception BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also used perception-based wiping completion. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with perception no longer clearly secondary BECAUSE earlier social meals used perception, some later social meals used autocontinue, and the newest front-social savory meal returned to perception. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and perception selected, BECAUSE the earlier front-social savory meal used autocontinue while the newer one used perception. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is favored BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with no clear winner BECAUSE some right-TV meals used autocontinue and others used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN perception BECAUSE all observed left-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the observed right-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using perception and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts more automation for wiping completion while working in front of a laptop. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice overall, while autocontinue appears especially in some screen-focused and social contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to add extra retraction motions. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal including chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup or bite-sized sandwich meal THEN no BECAUSE observed personal non-fruit meals also prioritized efficiency over extra retraction. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN yes and no are both established, with no now roughly as common as yes BECAUSE the user selected yes in some social meals but selected no in both observed front-social savory meals and other earlier social meals. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN no BECAUSE both observed front-social savory meals skipped retraction despite preserving front occlusion through other settings. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals selected retraction, consistent with preserving visibility of the front-facing screen. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date, including the new fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized efficiency over additional retraction in left-laptop work contexts. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized efficiency over additional retraction in right-laptop work contexts. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals selected retraction while attending to a front-facing screen. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both no and yes observed, and no remains slightly favored overall BECAUSE several earlier right-TV meals skipped retraction but a later right-TV meal selected retraction while preserving the right-side TV view. Across observed meals, the user usually prefers not to retract in personal, social-front, and side-work settings, but is more willing to retract when preserving a front-facing screen's visibility is especially salient.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and not selected. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND savory meal with ketchup such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the personal savory meal shows the user may want some sauce in personal settings without choosing heavy dipping. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND savory meal without a selected dipping sauce or bite-sized sandwich meal without a selected dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE other observed personal non-fruit meals used no dipping. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal with whipped cream or other available topping or dip THEN less BECAUSE the user selected restrained dipping in that social meal, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and the user did not select it. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed left-social sandwich meal did not add dipping. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized savory meal such as pizza and broccoli without a selected dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed front-social meal did not add dipping. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal with ranch dressing such as buffalo chicken bites and potato wedges THEN do not dip BECAUSE the newer front-social meal again declined dipping despite an available sauce. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips, including the new left-laptop fruit meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed work-context fruit meals involved no dipping. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup, tartar sauce, or another available dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE in observed work meals with available sauce the user explicitly chose not to add dipping, including repeated front-laptop fish-and-tartar meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND meal includes tartar sauce, ranch dressing, ketchup, chocolate sauce, or another available dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user repeatedly declined dipping in right-TV meals. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal or other meal with available sauce or dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed front-TV meals with sauce availability also used no dipping. Across observed meals, the user avoids dipping by default, with restrained dipping appearing in some personal and social sauce-available meals.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user previously preferred a longer pause before automatic continuation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli or chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup THEN 10 sec BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals used a short wait, indicating that in these meals the user prioritizes faster progression. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the personal sandwich meal returned to the longer wait. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec and 100 sec are both established, with 10 sec now slightly favored BECAUSE some earlier social meals used 100 sec, but both the front-social savory meals used 10 sec and newer left-social evidence is mixed. IF social dining context with a person in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as pizza and broccoli or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges and ranch dressing THEN 10 sec BECAUSE both observed front-social savory meals used the shortest wait. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used the longer wait. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec and 100 sec are both established, with no clear winner BECAUSE some right-TV meals used short waits while others used 100 sec. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals used the longer wait. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the new left-laptop fruit meal used the shortest wait. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec to 100 sec, with recent left-laptop evidence leaning shorter BECAUSE the user chose 10 sec in multiple left-laptop work meals, including the new fruit meal, but 100 sec in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both 10 sec and 100 sec selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers rapid automatic continuation in right-laptop work settings but not consistently across meals. Across observed contexts, the user often prefers longer waits in front-facing screen settings and many personal meals, while short waits appear in efficiency-oriented personal meals, are established in front-social savory meals, and now also recur in left-laptop work meals.
